data.title: Leonardo DRS targets $3.425B-$3.525B revenue in 2025 with 6%-9% organic growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Leonardo DRS, Inc. (NASDAQ:DRS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Bill Lynn, CEO, highlighted a strong 2024 performance with record bookings, mid-teens organic revenue growth, and a 23% increase in adjusted EBITDA. He attributed the success to robust international demand, improved supply chain conditions, and a diversified platform-agnostic portfolio. The company also secured $4 billion in contract awards and achieved a book-to-bill ratio of 1.3 for both the quarter and full year.

  • Lynn emphasized investments in growth-oriented initiatives, including a 25% increase in R&D and CapEx spending. Key areas of focus include directed energy capabilities, AI applications, and the development of a new Charleston, South Carolina facility to support the Columbia Class program and future platforms.

  • CFO Mike Dippold reported Q4 revenue of $981 million, reflecting a 6% year-over-year increase. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $148 million, marking a 13% growth, with margins expanding by 100 basis points. Full-year 2024 revenue reached $3.2 billion, a 14% annual increase, driven by strong performance across advanced infrared sensing, tactical radars, and electric power and propulsion businesses.

  • The board approved a $0.09 per share cash dividend and authorized a $75 million share buyback program over two years.

Outlook

  • Revenue guidance for 2025 is projected at $3.425 billion to $3.525 billion, implying organic growth of 6% to 9%. Management stated the backlog provides strong visibility for execution within this range.

  • Adjusted EBITDA is forecasted at $435 million to $455 million, with an expected margin improvement of 30 to 50 basis points. Adjusted diluted EPS is projected between $1.02 and $1.08, with a tax rate assumption of 19% and a fully diluted share count of 270 million.

  • Key growth drivers highlighted include the Columbia Class program, continued demand for tactical radars, and expanded international opportunities.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 adjusted diluted EPS grew 23% year-over-year, driven by operational performance and lower interest expense. Full-year adjusted diluted EPS rose by 27%.

  • Free cash flow for 2024 was $190 million, aligned with a targeted 80% conversion of adjusted net earnings. The company entered 2025 in a net cash position, supporting its balanced capital allocation strategy.

  • Advanced Sensing and Computing (ASC) segment revenue grew 9% in Q4 and 16% for the full year, supported by higher volumes and improved program execution. Integrated Mission Systems (IMS) segment revenue was down 1% in Q4 but increased 11% annually, aided by profitability on Columbia Class shipsets.

Q&A

  • Robert Stallard, Vertical Research, asked about potential effects of DOJ efforts on defense contracts. CEO Lynn clarified there had been no impact so far and emphasized focus on the Department of Defense’s strategic priorities.

  • Peter Arment, RW Baird, inquired about margin drivers beyond Columbia Class. Lynn pointed to smaller sensing and force protection programs transitioning from development to production, contributing to improved margins.

  • Andre Madrid, BTIG, asked about the impact of material supply restrictions, specifically on germanium. Management noted a stable supply but highlighted volatility in pricing as a concern.

  • Michael Ciarmoli, Truist Securities, questioned demand sustainability for counter-UAS systems. Lynn confirmed that technological advancements and international interest would sustain growth in this area.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a constructive tone, with questions focused on growth drivers, margin expansion, and risk management. Concerns centered on supply chain stability and geopolitical factors influencing defense investment.

  • Management maintained confidence in both prepared remarks and Q&A responses, emphasizing robust performance, backlog visibility, and proactive investments in innovation. CEO Lynn stressed the company’s ability to adapt to evolving customer needs and market conditions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance for 2025 was raised slightly from the Q3 2024 framework of 5%-8% growth to 6%-9%, reflecting strong backlog and improved execution.

  • Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion of 30 to 50 basis points in 2025 builds on the 90 basis points achieved in 2024. Management reinforced confidence in meeting longer-term targets, including the 14% margin goal by 2026.

  • Continued investments in R&D and CapEx were emphasized more in Q4, signaling a strategic focus on future growth opportunities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified the elongated lead times in the supply chain as a persistent challenge, although stabilization was noted.

  • Geopolitical risks, including potential shifts in U.S. defense spending priorities and international competition, were acknowledged as areas to monitor.

  • Analysts raised concerns about material availability, particularly germanium, and the potential impact of prolonged continuing resolutions on defense contracts.

Final Takeaway

Leonardo DRS delivered a strong close to 2024, marked by record bookings, double-digit revenue growth, and margin expansion. The company remains well-positioned to capitalize on robust defense demand, with a growing backlog and increasing international contributions. Management’s proactive investments in innovation, coupled with a balanced capital allocation strategy, provide a solid foundation for sustained growth in 2025 and beyond.

data.title: Dynavax projects HEPLISAV-B revenue of $305M-$325M for 2025 amid expanding hepatitis B market
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dynavax Technologies Corporation (NASDAQ:DVAX) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Ryan Spencer highlighted the company’s record HEPLISAV-B product revenue in Q4 and 2024, achieving profitability and implementing a share repurchase plan. Looking ahead, the company forecasts HEPLISAV-B net product sales between $305 million and $325 million for 2025, supported by an expanding U.S. hepatitis B vaccine market and expected market share gains.
  • The company plans to advance its Z1018 shingles vaccine program, aiming to establish proof of concept with Phase I/II results expected in Q3 2025. The program targets a multibillion-dollar market with a focus on improved tolerability compared to the current market leader.
  • Chief Commercial Officer Donn Casale emphasized HEPLISAV-B’s leadership in the U.S. hepatitis B adult vaccine market, achieving a 44% total market share by the end of 2024. The updated Medicare policy enabling retail access for hepatitis B vaccines and the inclusion of hepatitis B vaccination in HEDIS measures are expected to drive further market penetration in 2025.
  • CFO Kelly MacDonald reported HEPLISAV-B’s Q4 net sales of $71 million and full-year sales of $268 million, reflecting 26% annual growth. The gross margin for HEPLISAV-B reached 82% for 2024, and the company expects similar margins in 2025. She also introduced a new non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA measure for 2025, targeting at least $75 million.

Outlook

  • Dynavax expects HEPLISAV-B product revenue in 2025 to range between $305 million and $325 million, representing a 17% year-over-year growth at the midpoint.
  • The company anticipates achieving key clinical milestones for its Z1018 shingles vaccine in 2025, including Phase I/II data readouts and the commencement of a Phase II extension study in patients aged 70 and older.
  • The updated Medicare billing policy and HEDIS measure inclusion are significant drivers for HEPLISAV-B growth, particularly in the retail and IDN segments.

Financial Results

  • The company reported Q4 HEPLISAV-B net sales of $71 million compared to analysts' revenue estimate of $72.7 million. Full-year 2024 HEPLISAV-B revenue reached $268 million, up 26% from the prior year.
  • Gross margin for HEPLISAV-B improved to 82% in 2024, up from 76% in 2023, with similar levels expected in 2025.
  • Q4 net income was $7 million, contributing to a full-year net income of $27 million. Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA, excluding stock-based compensation, was $13 million for Q4 and $52 million for the full year, reflecting significant year-over-year growth.

Q&A

  • Matt Phipps, William Blair: Asked about barriers to driving HEPLISAV-B market share growth. Donn Casale highlighted two critical developments: Medicare’s updated billing policy and the inclusion of hepatitis B vaccination in HEDIS measures, which favor HEPLISAV-B due to its two-dose regimen.
  • Roy Buchanan, Citizens: Questioned the durability of shingles vaccine data and the logic behind the extension study in older adults. CEO Ryan Spencer and Chief Medical Officer Rob Janssen noted that while short-term durability cannot be inferred from one-month data, the six-month follow-up and extension study will provide further clarity.
  • Jonathan Miller, Evercore ISI: Inquired about capital allocation priorities, including share repurchases versus business development. CEO Spencer emphasized a balanced approach, with ongoing evaluation of external opportunities to complement internal growth.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised several forward-looking concerns, including HEPLISAV-B market share expansion, the durability of shingles vaccine data, and the company’s approach to capital allocation. The tone was largely neutral, with occasional positive sentiment on the company’s strategic positioning and growth drivers.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, using phrases like “we are confident” and “we expect,” but exhibited a slightly defensive posture during analyst exchanges, particularly around capital allocation and clinical trial strategies.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained consistent, though confidence in 2025 guidance and HEPLISAV-B’s growth trajectory appeared heightened.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance for 2025 increased from the narrowed 2024 range of $265 million-$270 million to $305 million-$325 million, reflecting optimism in market dynamics such as Medicare policy changes and HEDIS measure inclusion.
  • Strategic focus shifted slightly towards long-term pipeline development, including the Z1018 shingles vaccine program, with significant milestones expected in 2025.
  • Analyst sentiment remained focused on HEPLISAV-B’s market share growth and the shingles vaccine program, with recurring questions on data durability and safety differentiation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted potential challenges in achieving durable CD4 T cell responses for the shingles vaccine, which is critical for Phase III trial success.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about the scalability of HEPLISAV-B market share gains, particularly given competitive pressures and the need for sustained commercial efforts.
  • The company acknowledged the uncertainties in government assessments of the plague vaccine program’s market potential.

Final Takeaway

Dynavax delivered strong Q4 and full-year 2024 results, supported by record HEPLISAV-B revenue and improving gross margins. With a projected revenue range of $305 million-$325 million for 2025, the company is focused on market share expansion, leveraging favorable policy changes, and advancing its shingles vaccine program. Analysts remain attentive to the durability of growth drivers and clinical milestones, while management maintains confidence in its strategic initiatives and financial outlook.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nutrien says tariffs would hit U.S. farmers with higher costs
data.description:
Nutrien (NYSE:NTR) said Thursday that President Trump's proposed 25% tariffs on Canadian imports would raise costs for U.S. farmers, as they rely heavily on Canada for inputs such as potash.

Canada supplies more than 80% of the potash used on U.S. farms, and as Nutrien (NYSE:NTR) evaluates what tariffs could do to its business, "by far the biggest discussion is about potash," CEO Ken Seitz said on the company's earnings conference call.

"Frankly, costs of this [tariff] would be borne by the U.S. farmer... [who] will likely feel the impact after the spring planting season," Seitz said.

The CEO said U.S. farmers will be able to get fertilizer for the upcoming spring season, after weather problems in late 2024 prevented some planned fertilizer applications, leaving more needed in 2025; U.S. farmers are expected to plant at least 90M acres of fertilizer-hungry corn this spring, leaving the company optimistic about its 2025 U.S. demand outlook.

The company does not view the potential end of the Russia-Ukraine war as a threat to its sales in 2025, since Russian potash already has been reaching the market, Seitz added.

Nutrien (NTR) closed +1.3% on Thursday after reporting mixed Q4 results, as net profit fell to $118M, or $0.23/share, from $176M, or $0.35/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and total revenues fell 10% Y/Y to $5.1B, as potash sales sank 31% to $536M due to lower prices and lower sales volumes.

The company guided for FY 2025 potash sales volume of 13.6M-14.4M metric tons, accounting for some uncertainty over potential U.S. tariffs and global supply availability.

data.title: After hours movers: Rivian stock swings, Block stock dives
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) and Block (NYSE:XYZ) were on the move Thursday evening following financial updates from the EV maker and the company behind CashApp and Afterpay.

The regular trading session ended with losses for U.S. stocks, with sentiment hit by Walmart’s (WMT) financial projections that were below expectations. Walmart (WMT) fell 6.5%, the biggest decliner on the Dow (DJI). The blue-chips gauge fell more than 1%, and the S&P 500 (SP500) retreated from its record high set Wednesday.

Late movers:

Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) -0.6% after rising more than 1.3%, with volume surpassing 11 million shares. The company said it landed $170M in gross profit in Q4, notched record revenues, and narrowed its adjusted EBITDA loss. It did project deliveries of 46,000 to 51,000 vehicles in 2025 versus 51,579 in 2024.

Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH) +29% with the energy drink maker outstripping Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.14 by $0.04. It also confirmed that it's buying rival Alani Nu for $1.8 billion.

Block (NYSE:XYZ) -6.1% as the parent firm of Square and Cash App projected Q1 gross profit of $2.32B, less than the $2.38B Visible Alpha estimate. Q4 adjusted EPS missed Wall Street’s view.

S&P 500 (SP500) constituent Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) -6%, with 2025 adjusted EPS guidance of $6 to $6.40 from the content delivery networking company below expectations.

data.title: Osisko Gold Royalties outlines 2025 guidance with 80,000-88,000 GEOs and 97% cash margin
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Osisko Gold Royalties Ltd (NYSE:OR) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Jason Attew, President & CEO, highlighted that Osisko delivered 20,005 gold equivalent ounces (GEOs) in Q4 2024, ending the year at 80,740 GEOs, slightly above the midpoint of its revised guidance. The company achieved record revenues of $191.2 million with a leading cash margin of 96.5%.
  • Osisko ended the quarter with $59 million in cash and net debt of just under $35 million after paying down $85 million against its revolving credit facility during 2024.
  • The company committed to $300 million in transactions during 2024, including the Dalgaranga Royalty acquisition and Gibraltar Silver Stream amendments. Additionally, a quarterly dividend of $0.065 per share was declared for April 15, 2025.
  • Frederic Ruel, CFO, emphasized, "Our much improved balance sheet provides the company with the capacity and flexibility to continue its strategy of disciplined allocation."
  • Attew noted robust performance from the flagship Canadian Malartic Mine and the growing contribution from the CSA copper stream and MAC Copper assets.
  • The company expects meaningful growth from its Namdini mine starting in the second half of 2025 as well as additional contributions from Phase 3 expansion at Island Gold by 2026.

Outlook

  • Osisko provided 2025 guidance of 80,000 to 88,000 GEOs with a cash margin of approximately 97%.
  • The updated five-year outlook through 2029 reflects a reduced delivery range compared to prior projections due to the absence of Eagle Mine contributions. Growth will instead be fueled by expansions at Dalgaranga, Windfall, Hermosa, and Marimaca.
  • Management highlighted the back-half weighting of 2025 GEO deliveries, with approximately 55% expected in Q3 and Q4.
  • Attew remarked that "the slope of GEO delivery growth in 2025 is expected to be less steep than previously anticipated," due to adjustments at key assets such as Canadian Malartic and Mantos Blancos.

Financial Results

  • The company reported net earnings of $0.09 per share for 2024, a substantial year-over-year improvement. Adjusted annual earnings were $0.52 per share.
  • GEO deliveries in 2024 were predominantly derived from precious metals, with 67% from gold, 27% from silver, and the remainder from copper.
  • The Canadian Malartic Mine remained a top contributor, while Namdini poured its first gold in November 2024, positioning it as a future growth driver.
  • Mantos Blancos faced throughput challenges earlier in the year, but improvements in plant performance are expected to drive higher GEO deliveries starting in 2025.

Q&A

  • Tanya Jakusconek, Scotiabank: Asked about the 2025 production profile and whether Q1 and Q2 will see equal contributions. Jason Attew clarified that "Q1 will be the weakest quarter," with improvements expected in Q2 and a stronger performance in the second half of the year.
  • Tanya Jakusconek, Scotiabank: Inquired about capital allocation and transaction opportunities. Attew shared that Osisko is focusing on transactions in the $50 million to $500 million range and remains open to syndications for larger deals.
  • Adrian Day, Adrian Day Asset Management: Asked about the potential for swapping copper assets for gold. Attew responded that such a move would depend on whether it is accretive to shareholder value.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions about the company's production timelines and capital allocation, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone. Concerns were primarily operational and forward-looking.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing disciplined capital allocation and strong asset performance. Attew used assertive language such as "we are extremely proud" and "we remain optimistic."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management exhibited consistent confidence while addressing adjustments in growth expectations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted annual earnings increased from $0.15 per share in Q3 to $0.52 in FY 2024, driven by improved performance across key assets.
  • The 2025 guidance saw a reduction in projected growth slope compared to prior expectations, reflecting revised asset-specific profiles.
  • Analysts in the current quarter showed heightened interest in the timing of production ramp-ups and capital allocation strategies, compared to broader strategic queries in Q3.
  • Management maintained a consistent tone in both quarters, with a strong focus on executing disciplined growth strategies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged potential delays in ramp-ups at key assets, including Namdini and Canadian Malartic, as factors impacting 2025 growth.
  • Throughput challenges at Mantos Blancos were noted, with improvements expected only in the latter half of 2025.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about the competitive transaction environment and the implications for capital allocation.

Final Takeaway

Osisko Gold Royalties demonstrated robust performance in 2024, achieving record revenues and strong cash margins, while providing a realistic 2025 guidance range of 80,000 to 88,000 GEOs. The company remains focused on disciplined capital allocation and growth through high-quality transactions, despite adjusting expectations for asset ramp-ups. Investors should monitor developments at Canadian Malartic, Namdini, and Mantos Blancos, which are expected to drive near-term growth, alongside potential new transactions in 2025.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Grupo Televisa reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Grupo Televisa press release (NYSE:TV): Q4 GAAP EPS of -Ps.3.62
  • Revenues decreased by 5.2% to Ps.15,568.5 million compared with Ps.16,422.0 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.

  • Revenues in our Enterprise Operations decreased by 6.3%.
  • Finally, Sky’s revenues declined by 12.4%, driven by the year-on-year RGUs decrease.
